name: scrcpy
summary:
> [!WARNING]
> **This GitHub repo (<https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy>) is the only official
source for the project. Do not download releases from random websites, even if
their name contains `scrcpy`.**

their name contains `scrcpy`.**

function_signatures.name: index_entry_in_the_android_security_category_use_as_a_candidate_tool_in_the_chain_planner_after_a_nvd_lookup_pass_on_the_target_vendor
function_signatures.description: Index entry in the Android Security category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner after a NVD/lookup pass on the target vendor.

function_signatures.name: pairs_with_the_android_security_system_prompt_stanza_in_core_ai_backend_chain_to_teach_the_llm_the_standard_invocation_pattern
function_signatures.description: Pairs with the Android Security system-prompt stanza in core.ai_backend.chain to teach the LLM the standard invocation pattern.

function_signatures.name: operator_initiated_the_chain_step_is_gated_by_the_per_step_accept_cancel_gate_the_tool_does_not_auto_execute
function_signatures.description: Operator-initiated: the chain step is gated by the per-step ACCEPT/CANCEL gate; the tool does not auto-execute.

function_signatures.name: use_as_a_reference_implementation_when_designing_a_new_v3_method_for_this_category
function_signatures.description: Use as a reference implementation when designing a new v3 method for this category.

function_signatures.name: use_as_a_teaching_example_in_the_prompt_stanza_so_the_llm_can_reason_about_the_right_run_order
function_signatures.description: Use as a teaching example in the prompt stanza so the LLM can reason about the right run order.

function_signatures.name: main
function_signatures.description: Top-level entry point for scrcpy (unknown/scrcpy). Reads CLI args, dispatches to subcommands.

function_signatures.name: run
function_signatures.description: Top-level CLI runner for scrcpy. Returns a result envelope (ok, data, error).

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with unknown/drozer (score=12); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with unknown/jadx (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with unknown/frida (score=11); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['shell_linux', 'shell_android'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 4: same attack_surface=['shell_linux', 'shell_android'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 5: same attack_surface=['shell_linux', 'shell_android'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use this tool when the engagement is in the 'any' phase against a target with '['shell_linux', 'shell_android']' as the attack surface (android_security, toolbox, github).
documentation.why_to_use: This tool is preferred because: Index entry in the Android Security category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner after a NVD/lookup pass on the target vendor.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: Index entry in the Android Security category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner after a NVD/lookup pass on the target vendor.
Pairs with the Android Security system-prompt stanza in core.ai_backend.chain to teach the LLM the standard invocation pattern.
Operator-initiated: the chain step is gated by the per-step ACCEPT/CANCEL gate; the tool does not auto-execute.
Use as a reference implementation when designing a new v3 method for this category.
Use as a teaching example in the prompt stanza so the LLM can reason about the right run order.
